title: Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Profiles
text:
Background on the Issue
Ecosystem-based science is a primary component of effective marine conservation and resource management, and NOAA Fisheries is committed to ecosystem-based management as our most powerful tool to maintain productive and resilient fisheries. This approach considers the entire ecosystem, including humans, to balance tradeoffs between ecological, social, and economic needs.
Our national standard guidelines require the consideration of ecosystem and socioeconomic processes with regard to specifying optimum yield. While progress has been made via comprehensive ecosystem status reports, a gap remains between conducting ecosystem research and integrating it within the fishery management process. Primary obstacles include a lack of a consistent approach to deciding when and how to test and incorporate this information.

title: Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Profiles
text:
History of ESPs
The ESP was first conceived in 2014, building on NOAA Fisheries’ rich history of identifying ecosystem and socioeconomic pressures since the 1990s.

2017: The first ESP case study using Alaska sablefish was completed.
2018–Present: Developed and refined through the North Pacific Fisheries Management Council review process and recommendations from the Next Generation Stock Assessment Improvement Plan.
Current: ESPs are now being adapted and adopted across the nation with the help of a National ESP Coordination Team.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
text:
How do ESPs relate to the Integrated Ecosystem Assessment (IEA) program?
[cite_start]ESPs are part of the larger, iterative IEA approach[cite: 113]. [cite_start]They function as a tool to implement the IEA loop by translating large-scale indicators down to stock-specific applications[cite: 113, 114].


How do ESPs support the stock assessment program?
[cite_start]ESPs are directly responsive to the proposed 3-step process outlined in the Implementing a Next Generation Stock Assessment Enterprise framework (NG-SAIP; Lynch 2018)[cite: 116]. [cite_start]They facilitate more robust, accurate, and ecosystem-linked stock assessments[cite: 117].


How do ESPs support EBFM?
[cite_start]They directly accelerate the adoption of Ecosystem-Based Fisheries Management by cleanly intersecting social science, ecological indicators, and actual management policy advice[cite: 119].


How can ESPs support CEFI?
[cite_start]ESPs aid the operational production of climate-informed advice[cite: 121]. [cite_start]They serve as clear delivery pipelines bringing ocean and climate predictive outputs developed in CEFI directly into evaluation rooms[cite: 122].

title: National Coordination
text:
[cite_start]The increase in momentum across all six science centers to produce ESPs or ESP-like analysis inspired the creation of the National ESP initiative in 2022, followed by the formation of the National ESP Coordination Team in 2023[cite: 87].

“And now we are going national with dedicated workshops to share ESP development across the country… We’ve developed the foundation. It’s malleable. It can be adapted.” [cite_start]— Kalei Shotwell (Alaska Fisheries Science Center) [cite: 107, 108]



[cite_start]To help launch, streamline, and coordinate the ESP process at all six science centers[cite: 89]. [cite_start]We enable a unified framework to integrate ecosystem and socioeconomic research within our stock assessment enterprise, providing an accounting mechanism to show progress toward ecosystem-based fisheries management (EBFM)[cite: 89].




[cite_start]Web Maintenance: Maintain this centralized space reviewing the ESP process and connecting reference data[cite: 93].
[cite_start]Step-by-Step Guides: Develop and maintain a step-by-step guide for initiating and developing regional profiles[cite: 94].
[cite_start]National ESP Symposium: Coordinate annual/biennial forums and workshops for cross-center sharing and innovation panels[cite: 96].
[cite_start]Training & Development: Facilitate ongoing technical training ranging from brief seminars to multi-day co-creation hackathons[cite: 98].
[cite_start]Tracking Progress: Track region status metrics, prioritized fish stocks, and real-world management use cases[cite: 100].

title: National Coordination

[cite_start]The increase in momentum across all six science centers to produce ESPs or ESP-like analysis inspired the creation of the National ESP initiative in 2022, followed by the formation of the National ESP Coordination Team in 2023[cite: 87].

“And now we are going national with dedicated workshops to share ESP development across the country… We’ve developed the foundation. It’s malleable. It can be adapted.” [cite_start]— Kalei Shotwell (Alaska Fisheries Science Center) [cite: 107, 108]

[cite_start]Tracking Progress: Track region status metrics, prioritized fish stocks, and real-world management use cases[cite: 100].

title: The 4 Steps of an ESP
text: [cite_start]The ESP integrates ecosystem and socioeconomic factors into the stock assessment process through a four-step framework: Focus, Synthesis, Analyze, and Communicate[cite: 30].
